metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a file-scope assertion to AC5's proving command that enumerates changed paths and asserts none fall outside the permitted set (`.rebar/`, criterion test file, `docs/llm-framework.md`, `.gitignore`), so the 'only' exclusivity claim is actually measured.
evidence: AC5 text: 'The criterion is entirely project-owned: its implementation diff changes only committed `.rebar/` prompt/routing/eval assets, their `.gitignore` exceptions, criterion-specific tests, and the `Project dogfood` subsection of `docs/llm-framework.md`'

Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py && .venv/bin/python -c 'from pathlib import Path; s=Path("docs/llm-framework.md").read_text(); assert "Project dogfood: review-phase-boundaries" in s' && make lint`
ABSENCE: no command enumerates changed files or asserts no unexpected paths appear in the diff — the 'only' exclusivity claim is untested.
findings.finding: AC5's proving command cannot verify the 'only' constraint it claims to enforce. The AC states the diff changes ONLY the listed `.rebar/`, test, and docs paths, but the verification command (`pytest` + substring check + `make lint`) confirms presence of expected artifacts and lint cleanliness — not the absence of unexpected file modifications. A diff that also touches a kernel or other file would still pass all three commands, leaving the 'entirely project-owned' guarantee unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (`The criterion is entirely project-owned…`)
scenarios: An executor accidentally modifies a shared kernel file alongside the new `.rebar/` assets; AC5's proving command passes (pytest green, string present, lint clean) yet the ownership contract is violated.
A reviewer or CI audit later relies on AC5 as evidence of clean project-only scope and finds undisclosed kernel changes.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to AC5's verify block:
```bash
git diff --name-only HEAD~1 \
  | grep -Ev '^(\.rebar/|tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries\.py|docs/llm-framework\.md|\.gitignore)' \
  | { read line && echo "UNEXPECTED FILE: $line" && exit 1 || exit 0; }
```
This exits non-zero if any changed file falls outside the permitted set, making the 'entirely project-owned' claim falsifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a must-not-fire corpus case (e.g. RP-N7 or repurpose a slot) where the changed instruction cannot be attributed to a specific pass, and assert the criterion abstains (emits no finding).
evidence: Scope: 'Guards that distinguish operative instructions from documentation or negative examples, require identifiable phase ownership, and abstain when ownership is unclear.'

Eval Contract: RP-N1 covers narrowing problem class; RP-N2 covers strengthening evidence rules; RP-N3 covers Pass-2 atomic questions; RP-N4 covers threshold changes; RP-N5 covers Pass-4 next-move; RP-N6 covers documentation/grandfathered debt. None of the six must-not-fire cases is explicitly mapped to 'diff where phase ownership is ambiguous → abstain → no finding.'
ABSENCE: no must-not-fire case description mentions unclear phase ownership or the abstention path.
findings.finding: The abstention guard — 'abstain when phase ownership is unclear' — is specified in Scope but has no dedicated eval corpus case validating that the criterion correctly emits no finding when a diff's changed instruction cannot be attributed to a specific phase.
findings.location: Scope section ('Guards that distinguish…abstain when ownership is unclear') and Eval Contract (RP-N1..RP-N6 case descriptions)
scenarios: A diff modifies a shared preamble or shared-utilities section that could be read as either Pass-1 or Pass-2 guidance; the criterion should abstain but this behavior is never validated by the corpus.
An evaluator tuning the prompt could silently break the abstention guard without any corpus case catching the regression.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a case like: `RP-N7: a diff modifies a shared/preamble instruction that does not identify which pass owns it; expect no finding (criterion abstains).` Wire it into the eval YAML and update the unit test to assert abstention behavior on an unclear-ownership diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verification step (in AC2's pytest assertions or a dedicated assertion) that the prompt file `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` contains `execution_mode: single_turn`.
evidence: Scope: 'Use routing `exec: "1-TURN"` and prompt `execution_mode: single_turn`'
AC1 assertion checks only routing JSON: `r["exec"] == "1-TURN"`
AC2 test assertion checks 'grounded evidence is present, severity/confidence are absent, and suggested_fix is absent or empty' — no mention of execution_mode
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly requires 'prompt `execution_mode: single_turn`' in the prompt file, but no acceptance criterion verifies this field is present in `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md`. AC1 only checks the routing JSON for `exec: "1-TURN"`; AC2's test asserts phase-boundary guards and absence of severity/confidence/suggested_fix but says nothing about `execution_mode`.
findings.location: Scope section ('Use routing `exec: "1-TURN"` and prompt `execution_mode: single_turn`') vs. AC1 and AC2
scenarios: An implementer sets exec in routing but forgets execution_mode in the prompt file; all ACs pass but the described contract is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the `test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py` assertions to read the prompt file and assert `execution_mode: single_turn` is present, or add an inline check to AC2's verify command.

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement (log/metric/trace) for the new `1-TURN` LLM call so that timeouts, malformed responses, and provider errors are surfaced to operators rather than silently swallowed.
evidence: Plan adds `exec: "1-TURN"` routing for the new criterion — a live LLM call — but contains no mention of logging, metrics, tracing, or alerting for that call's failure modes.
ABSENCE: no observability requirement appears in any AC, the eval contract, or the scope section.
The existing `_run_code_review_case` infrastructure is referenced but its observability properties are not confirmed to cover this new criterion.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new LLM call path (the Pass-1 finder executed via `exec: "1-TURN"`) but specifies no observability instrumentation — no log, metric, trace, or alert — for failures of that call. If the finder times out, returns malformed output, or the model is unavailable, operators have no signal to diagnose why the criterion produced no findings.
findings.location: Scope section / `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` activation entry / Acceptance Criteria (AC 1)
scenarios: The LLM provider is unavailable during a code-review run; the criterion silently produces zero findings; no operator alert fires; the advisory finding is permanently lost with no trace.
A malformed model response causes the criterion to return no structured output; the eval reports 0 recall but the root cause (bad response vs. genuine miss) is indistinguishable without logs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace hardcoded `.venv/bin/` prefixes in all `verify with` commands with a portable invocation (e.g., `python -m pytest`, `python -m rebar`, or a `make` target) so the ACs are executable on Windows as well as POSIX platforms.
evidence: `.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; d=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json")); ...'`
`.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json | .venv/bin/python -c '...'`
`.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py`
Windows is an explicitly listed supported platform in the rebar portability matrix; Python venvs on Windows use `.venv\Scripts\python.exe`, not `.venv/bin/python`.
findings.finding: All five AC verification commands use `.venv/bin/python` and `.venv/bin/rebar` POSIX paths and rely on POSIX shell pipe syntax (`|`). On Windows, a virtual environment places executables in `.venv\Scripts\`, not `.venv/bin/`, so the mandated verification commands fail before any logic runs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five `verify with` commands (e.g., `.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; ...'`, `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval ... | .venv/bin/python -c '...'`)
scenarios: Windows platform: a developer or CI runner on Windows attempts to run the mandated `verify with` command; the shell reports `'.venv' is not recognized` or `No such file or directory` because `.venv/bin/` does not exist on Windows, making the AC unverifiable without undocumented path substitution.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `.venv/bin/pytest` with `python -m pytest`, `.venv/bin/python` with `python`, and `.venv/bin/rebar` with `python -m rebar` (or wrap in a `make verify-225a` target that resolves the venv path per platform), so the verification commands are identical across POSIX and Windows CI.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `project.review-phase-boundaries` should be registered under the `plan_review` gate (consistent with `project.portability` and the existing eval path via `_criterion_id` → `_run_criterion_case`) or whether adding a new `eval_solver` arm for `code_review`-gated project criteria is in-scope; the plan currently claims to reuse `_run_code_review_case` but the existing routing in `eval_solver.py:162-176` blocks all `project.*`-namespaced ids.
evidence: Plan Eval Contract: 'criteria eval resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing _run_code_review_case per-prompt arm, which supplies the diff as ticket_context'
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:162-176: `_code_review_prompt_id` only accepts `code-review-{OVERLAY_IDS}` — no `project.*` namespace handled
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:66-88: `_criterion_id` resolves against `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)` (plan_review gate only); a code_review-only criterion is invisible

src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42: `criterion_prompt_id('project.review-phase-boundaries')` → `'plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'` — starts with `plan-review-`, not `code-review-`, so `_code_review_prompt_id` returns None

The existing project criterion `project.portability` is registered under `plan_review` gate (.rebar/criteria_routing.json), and its eval works via the `_criterion_id` → `_run_criterion_case` path; no parallel path exists for `code_review`-gated project criteria
findings.finding: The plan registers the criterion under the `code_review` gate but then claims to reuse `eval_solver`'s existing `_run_code_review_case` arm — an arm that only admits the 13 closed `OVERLAY_IDS` (plus `code-review-base`/`code-review-verify`). The physical prompt id `criterion_prompt_id` generates is `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` (starts with `plan-review-`, not `code-review-`), so `_code_review_prompt_id` returns `None` and the code-review arm is never reached. The `_criterion_id` arm that would handle it resolves only against `plan_review.registry.by_id`, which reads the `plan_review` gate — a criterion registered exclusively under `code_review` is invisible to it. The only existing project criterion (`project.portability`) works through the `plan_review` gate, not `code_review`. There is no existing eval-solver arm that can route a `code_review`-gated project criterion; the plan describes reusing a path that structurally cannot serve it, rather than using the already-proven `plan_review` project-criterion path.
findings.location: Eval Contract section: 'criteria eval resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing _run_code_review_case per-prompt arm'; AC line for `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` activation: `d["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]`
scenarios: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3` invokes `calibrate_criterion`, which calls `eval_solver.run_case('plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries', case, ...)`. The `_criterion_id` arm returns None (criterion not in plan_review registry). The `_code_review_prompt_id` arm returns None (id doesn't start with `code-review-`). The agentic fallback arms don't match. `run_case` raises `ValueError: unknown …` and the eval fails before a single case runs.

The AC verification script `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json | python -c '...'` would never produce the expected JSON payload, making the operator-attested live eval impossible to record.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that dependency story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` is **closed and landed on main** before running the AC3 eval command, or explicitly call out in AC3 that `rebar criteria eval` and `calibrate_criterion` require the gate-keyed extensions that story delivers (gate-aware `_code_review_prompt_id`, `calibrate_criterion(gate_key=...)`, and multi-gate `criteria eval` preflight).
evidence: Plan Eval Contract: 'criteria eval resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing _run_code_review_case per-prompt arm, which supplies the diff as ticket_context'
_criteria_eval in src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py line 147: `if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root)` — only checks plan_review.registry

_code_review_prompt_id in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 169-176: `valid = {'code-review-base', 'code-review-verify'} | {f'code-review-{oid}' for oid in OVERLAY_IDS}` — project ids not in valid set
calibrate_criterion in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py line 419: `prompt_id = criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — no gate_key, always returns plan-review- prefix

Dependency ticket 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 (open): 'Extend `_code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id, repo_root)` to recognize the sanitized physical id only when its logical inverse is active, then reuse the existing `_run_code_review_case` arm' — this extension does not exist yet
Dependency ticket 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 Scope: 'Extend calibration as `calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runs=1, gate_key="plan_review")`' — this parameter does not exist yet
Dependency ticket 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 Scope: 'Extend `criteria eval` preflight to accept a logical id only when it is active in code review's repository-effective criteria' — not yet implemented
findings.finding: The Eval Contract section asserts '`criteria eval` resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm' as if this capability exists today, but three verified code barriers currently prevent it: (1) `_criteria_eval` checks only `plan_review.registry.by_id()`, so `project.review-phase-boundaries` (a code-review-gate criterion) fails immediately as 'unknown criterion'; (2) `calibrate_criterion` has no `gate_key` parameter and always maps to the `plan-review-` prefix, routing through `_run_criterion_case` (plan-review Pass-1 arm that reads `case['input']`, not `case['diff']`); (3) `_code_review_prompt_id` accepts only `OVERLAY_IDS` members plus `code-review-base`/`code-review-verify` — project ids are rejected. The open dependency ticket `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` explicitly describes adding each of these missing capabilities (gate-keyed `criterion_prompt_id`, gate-aware `calibrate_criterion`, extended `_code_review_prompt_id`, and multi-gate `criteria eval` preflight). The plan's AC3 verify command (`rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`) and the eval corpus calibration both rely on these not-yet-landed extensions as pre-existing infrastructure.
findings.location: Eval Contract section; AC3 verify command; Dependency section
scenarios: Running `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3` on current code fails immediately with 'unknown criterion' before any eval fixture is touched, making AC3 unverifiable.

Even if the criterion were in the registry, `calibrate_criterion` would call `eval_solver.run_case('plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries', case)` which routes through the plan-review `_run_criterion_case` arm reading `case['input']` — but eval cases use `diff` key, so every case would run on empty input and produce wrong fire/no-fire results.
The 36-execution live eval required by the operator-attested AC4 cannot be completed until `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` is closed and its changes landed on main.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a preflight check to AC3: 'Prerequisite: `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` landed. Verify with `git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02'`.' Alternatively, rewrite the AC3 verify command to reflect the post-seam invocation shape: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --gate code_review --runs 3 --output json` (matching the `gate_key` parameter `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` will add).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the prompt file to `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` and eval spec to `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` throughout the plan (Scope, AC2, AC3, AC5, and the `.gitignore` exception list) to match `criterion_prompt_id('project.review-phase-boundaries')` → `'plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'`.
evidence: Plan Scope: 'A committed project prompt at `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md`' and 'balanced eval spec at `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`'
`src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` line 42: `return f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` where `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'plan-review-'`
`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py` line 411-413: 'a project.<name> criterion's fixture is `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-<name>.eval.yaml`'
Existing analogue: `.gitignore` lines 24+27 show `!.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md` and `!.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`
`tests/unit/test_eval_solver_project_criterion.py` line 79: `(rebar_dir / 'evals' / 'plan-review-project-testcrit.eval.yaml')`
findings.finding: The plan names the prompt file `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` and the eval spec `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, but `criterion_prompt_id('project.review-phase-boundaries')` (the deterministic mapping in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`) produces `'plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'`. All project criteria use the `plan-review-` prefix, not `code-review-`: the existing analogue `project.portability` is at `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md` and `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`. Using the wrong prefix means `calibrate_criterion` will look for the wrong eval spec path, the prompt loader won't resolve the criterion, and the `.gitignore` exceptions must also be corrected.
findings.location: Scope section (prompt and eval spec file paths); AC2 (`test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py`); AC3 (`rebar criteria eval` command); AC5 (`docs/llm-framework.md` verification)
scenarios: When `calibrate_criterion('project.review-phase-boundaries', ...)` is called, it derives `prompt_id = 'plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'` and looks for `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` — the `code-review-` prefixed file the plan names will never be found, causing an `EvalError`.
AC3's `rebar criteria eval` command and AC4's operator-attested run will both fail because the eval spec is at the wrong path.
The `.gitignore` exceptions the plan says to add for the `code-review-` prefixed files won't git-track the actual deliverable files, leaving them ignored.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace every occurrence of `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` (as a filename prefix) with `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the Scope section, AC2, AC3, AC5, and any `.gitignore` exception entries. This matches the deterministic `criterion_prompt_id()` mapping used by `calibrate_criterion` and the prompt loader.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `project.review-phase-boundaries` under the `plan_review` key (or a shared key) in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` (in addition to or instead of `code_review`), OR update the `rebar criteria eval` CLI guard to also check `code_review.registry.effective_criteria()`, so the AC3/AC4 eval command can resolve the criterion.
evidence: `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py` lines 144-155: `if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root): sys.stderr.write(f"Error: unknown criterion...")`
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` line 211: `effective_criteria` delegates to shared overlay with `gate_key='plan_review'`
`src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` lines 305-322: when an `activate` id is defined only in another gate, `in_another_gate = True` → `continue` (NOT added to plan_review effective criteria)
The plan's AC1 check: `d['code_review']['project.review-phase-boundaries']` — placing the criterion under `code_review` only
Existing project.portability analogue in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`: uses `plan_review` key: `{"plan_review": {"project.portability": {...}}, "activate": [...]}`
findings.finding: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries` — the AC3 and AC4 verification command — guards against unknown criteria by checking `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)` (line 147 of `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`). That registry is populated only from criteria activated in the `plan_review` section of `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`. The plan routes `project.review-phase-boundaries` exclusively under the `code_review` key; the `effective_criteria` logic (confirmed in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` lines 305-322) silently skips such criteria for the `plan_review` gate. As a result, the CLI will reject the criterion as 'unknown' and exit non-zero before any eval runs.
findings.location: AC1 (`criteria_routing.json` activation check); AC3 (`rebar criteria eval` command); AC4 (operator-attested live run); Scope ('Routing with `block_threshold: 0.90` and `blocking_enabled: false`')
scenarios: Running `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` (AC3) will print 'Error: unknown criterion ...' and exit 1 because the plan_review registry doesn't include a code_review-only criterion.
The AC4 operator-attested three-run live eval cannot be recorded because the command always fails at the CLI guard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add to the dependency story's scope) that `_criteria_eval`'s plan-review-only registry gate and `calibrate_criterion`'s hard-coded `plan-review-` prefix mapping are both patched to recognize activated `code_review` project criteria, so `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` can reach `_run_code_review_case` end-to-end.
evidence: Plan: 'reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm, which supplies the diff as `ticket_context` and scores the native `{findings: [...]}` output'
eval.py:416-420: `prompt_id = criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — criterion_prompt_id always prepends 'plan-review-'
_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:147: `if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root)` — checks plan_review registry only
eval_solver.py:162-176: `_code_review_prompt_id` returns None for any id not in `OVERLAY_IDS | {base, verify}`

Dependency story 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 Scope: extends `criterion_prompt_id` with `gate_key` and `_code_review_prompt_id` to recognize the sanitized physical id — but the CLI plan-review-only gate check is not explicitly called out as a fix target
findings.finding: The Eval Contract section asserts the eval path 'reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm,' but three current code barriers silently block that path for a `project.review-phase-boundaries` logical id: (1) `calibrate_criterion` in `eval.py` unconditionally maps logical→physical with the `plan-review-` prefix (producing `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`, not `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`); (2) the CLI's `_criteria_eval` checks only the plan-review `registry.by_id()` and returns 'unknown criterion' before execution; (3) `_code_review_prompt_id` in `eval_solver.py` only recognizes `OVERLAY_IDS` members plus base/verify, so the new physical id returns `None` and the `_run_code_review_case` branch is never reached. Story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` extends `criterion_prompt_id` and `_code_review_prompt_id`, but the CLI registry gate and `calibrate_criterion` gate-key threading are not verified as explicitly in scope for that story.
findings.location: Eval Contract section; also AC line 3 (`rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`)
scenarios: Developer runs `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` as specified in AC line 3; the CLI returns 'Error: unknown criterion' because it checks only the plan-review registry, making the operator-attested eval impossible until the seam story lands and the CLI is patched.

Even after the seam story's `criterion_prompt_id(gate_key='code_review')` fix lands, if `calibrate_criterion` is not also updated to pass `gate_key='code_review'`, it maps to `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` and raises an EvalError for a missing eval spec.

evidence: Plan: 'criteria eval resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing _run_code_review_case per-prompt arm'

Evidence class: Partially-verified (the `_run_code_review_case` arm exists; the routing to it for a code-review project criterion is absent/unconfirmed)
findings.finding: The eval solver has no existing routing arm that connects a `code_review`-gate project criterion (e.g. `project.review-phase-boundaries`, physical prompt id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`) to `_run_code_review_case`. `run_case` dispatches via `_criterion_id()` (checks plan-review registry only) and `_code_review_prompt_id()` (checks the closed `OVERLAY_IDS` tuple only); neither matches a project criterion under the code-review gate. The plan's Eval Contract claims it 'reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm' but no such routing path currently exists for this criterion type.
findings.location: Eval Contract section; AC criterion 3 (`rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`); `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` lines 66–88, 162–176, 212–255
scenarios: Running `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` falls through `run_case` to the agentic store-reader path (which spins up a disposable git store), producing an incorrect result or error, causing the AC assertion to fail.
The live three-run eval recorded on the ticket cannot pass if the eval solver does not route the criterion to the right arm.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete eval routing path for a `code_review`-gate project criterion: document whether `_code_review_prompt_id` must be extended to admit `project.*` ids, or whether `calibrate_criterion`/the CLI must be updated to consult `code_review.registry`, and confirm that `case['diff']` is mapped to the input key the execution arm reads.
evidence: Plan: 'criteria eval resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm, which supplies the diff as `ticket_context`'
Plan: 'Each YAML case uses the `diff` key'
`_code_review_prompt_id()` valid set: `{'code-review-base', 'code-review-verify'} | {f'code-review-{oid}' for oid in OVERLAY_IDS}` — a hardcoded closed set that excludes any `project.*` id
`rebar criteria eval` CLI checks `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)` and rejects any criterion not in that registry with 'unknown criterion'
`calibrate_criterion()` calls `criterion_prompt_id('project.review-phase-boundaries')` → `'plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'`, not `'code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries'`
`_run_criterion_case` reads `case.get('input')`, not `case.get('diff')` — the `diff` key specified by the eval contract is silently ignored
findings.finding: The Eval Contract asserts the criterion 'reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm' and that 'Each YAML case uses the `diff` key' — but no such routing path exists for a `code_review`-gate project criterion. `_code_review_prompt_id()` accepts only the 13 closed `OVERLAY_IDS`-derived ids plus `code-review-base`/`code-review-verify`; `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` is not in that closed set and the function returns `None`. Additionally, `rebar criteria eval` validates against `plan_review.registry`, not `code_review.registry`, so the CLI rejects the criterion as unknown before any eval runs. The `diff` key is also never read by `_run_criterion_case` (it reads `case.get('input')`). No defined processing protocol connects a `code_review`-gate project criterion to the diff-based eval arm.
scenarios: Running `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3` fails immediately at the CLI guard with 'unknown criterion' because the criterion lives in `code_review.registry`, not `plan_review.registry`.

If the CLI guard is bypassed, `calibrate_criterion` computes `criterion_prompt_id` → `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`, looks for `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, and fails with EvalError because no such file exists (the plan names the file `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`).

If somehow the code-review arm is reached, `_code_review_prompt_id('code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries')` returns `None` (not in OVERLAY_IDS), so the run_case dispatcher falls through to the agentic case_store arm and crashes on the inline diff text.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
